title: Lepigonum macrothecum
taxonomy: Lepigonum macrothecum is classified within the plant family Santalaceae. This plant family is a group of parasitic flowering plants commonly known as sandalwoods or mistletoes. Lepigonum macrothecum is native to New Caledonia and is an evergreen shrub or small tree. The classification of Lepigonum macrothecum is further narrowed down to the genus Lepigonum, which consists of several species found in New Caledonia. Lepigonum macrothecum is a part of the order Santalales, which is a group of parasitic flowering plants.
uses: Lepigonum macrothecum is a plant species native to the southwestern Pacific. There is limited information available on its medicinal uses.
